justification: All four agents' contributions are represented with attribution. Pippen's fail-open behavior and both metrics (redis_unavailable_total, rate_limit_exceeded_total) are included. Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly stated. Both ACs are present with correct values (AC1: 101st request rejected, AC2: window reset case at T=61s). However, Kobe's sorted set recommendation is conflated with MJ's architecture decision — the brief presents sorted sets as THE chosen approach and states 'MJ's key-per-window approach is discarded,' when the rubric requires Kobe's recommendation to be framed as a recommendation distinct from MJ's decision. The brief treats Kobe's suggestion as having overridden MJ's architecture, rather than preserving the distinction between MJ's sliding window counter decision and Kobe's alternative suggestion. Review notes are also not kept distinct from architecture guidance — Kobe's concerns are woven directly into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than appearing in a separate review_notes section.

observations.text: All four agents (Bird, MJ, Kobe, Pippen) are clearly represented with specific attribution throughout the brief
Pippen's fail-open requirement is fully captured with testable assertion and dedicated acceptance criterion
Both required metrics included: redis_unavailable_total and rate_limit_exceeded_total
Dynamic Retry-After computation explicitly stated with formula: (oldest_score + 60) - now
Both ACs present with correct values including the window reset case (T=61s with 50 expired requests)
Excellent terminology_alignment and contradictions_resolved sections showing synthesis work

Kobe's sorted set recommendation is presented as the chosen architecture, not as a recommendation — the brief says 'Use Redis sorted sets (NOT fixed-window counters)' and 'MJ's key-per-window approach is discarded', which conflates Kobe's suggestion with the architectural decision
No distinct review_notes section — Kobe's concerns about boundary burst and fail-open testing are embedded in architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than kept separate
Magic overstepped by resolving the MJ vs Kobe tension unilaterally instead of preserving Kobe's input as a recommendation for Shaq to consider or escalating the architectural disagreement

trials.justification: All four agents' contributions are present with clear attribution, ACs are correct, fail-open and both metrics are included, and dynamic Retry-After is explicit. However, Magic conflates Kobe's sorted-set recommendation with MJ's architecture decision: the brief presents sorted sets as THE chosen approach ('Use Redis sorted sets for a true sliding window, NOT fixed window counters') rather than preserving MJ's sliding window counter as the architecture decision and framing Kobe's sorted-set suggestion as a recommendation to consider. The contradictions_resolved section says 'Adopted Kobe's recommendation', effectively overriding MJ's decision rather than surfacing the tension for Shaq. There is also no distinct review_notes section — Kobe's concerns are folded into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than kept separate.

observations.text: All four agents (Bird, MJ, Pippen, Kobe) are represented with explicit attribution in task_context and throughout the brief
Domain rules correctly capture 100 req/60s, 429 with dynamic Retry-After, and fail-open behavior
Both ACs present: AC1 (101st request rejected) and AC2 (window reset at t=61s allowing new requests)
Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly described with formula: ceiling((oldest_entry_timestamp + 60000 - now) / 1000)
Pippen's fail-open requirement preserved with both metrics (rate_limit_exceeded_total, redis_unavailable_total)
Kobe's fail-open test requirement explicitly called out

Kobe's sorted-set recommendation is presented as the chosen architecture ('Use Redis sorted sets... NOT fixed window counters'), overriding MJ's sliding window counter decision rather than framing it as a recommendation for Shaq to consider
No distinct review_notes section — Kobe's review concerns are merged into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved, losing the separation between architecture decisions and review feedback
Magic acts as an arbiter resolving the MJ-vs-Kobe tension unilaterally rather than preserving the distinction and letting the team or Shaq understand both perspectives

trials.justification: All four agents' contributions are represented with attribution. Both ACs (101st request rejection and window reset) are present with correct values. Pippen's fail-open behavior and both metrics (rate_limit_exceeded_total, redis_unavailable_total) are included. Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly stated. However, Kobe's sorted set recommendation is conflated with MJ's architecture decision — the brief presents sorted sets as THE chosen approach ('Use Redis sorted sets for a true sliding window') rather than preserving MJ's sliding window counter as the primary decision with Kobe's sorted set suggestion framed as a recommendation. The contradictions_resolved section explicitly says 'Adopt Kobe's recommendation' and replaces MJ's design, rather than keeping review notes distinct from architecture guidance. There is no separate review_notes section; Kobe's concerns are folded directly into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved, eliminating the distinction between MJ's decision and Kobe's suggestion.

observations.text: All four agents (Bird, MJ, Kobe, Pippen) are clearly attributed throughout the brief
Both ACs present: 101st request rejection and window reset at t=61s with correct values
Pippen's fail-open behavior explicitly included with redis_unavailable_total metric and WARN logging
Dynamic Retry-After computation clearly specified as ceil(oldest_score + 60 - now)
Both required metrics (rate_limit_exceeded_total, redis_unavailable_total) are present
Added a bonus AC for boundary burst prevention, showing thorough synthesis

Kobe's sorted set recommendation is presented as the chosen approach, not as a recommendation — violates the requirement to preserve the distinction between MJ's decision (sliding window counter) and Kobe's suggestion (sorted sets)
No separate review_notes section — Kobe's review concerns are merged into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than kept distinct
The brief overwrites MJ's architecture rather than framing Kobe's input as advisory, which misrepresents the authority relationship between architect and reviewer
